title: festuca cyllenica subsp. thasia
intro: Festuca cyllenica subsp. thasia, also known as Cretan blue fescue, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Festuca cyllenica subsp. thasia include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections. The plant can be prepared as an infusion or decoction, or used in herbal preparations such as creams and salves, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine.
